briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. Welcome to the climate briefing for May 15, 2026. We will now begin.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Climate Crisis and Its Domestic and International Impacts
sections.content: News released today warns of a deepening global climate crisis. Domestically, heavy rainfall is occurring frequently in the Antarctic Ocean [1], and aquaculture farms in Jeollanam-do are facing shortened periods of high water temperatures and increased disasters due to climate change, prompting encouragement for disaster insurance enrollment [5, 11]. Furthermore, beyond monitoring abnormal weather and heatwaves threatening facility horticulture, the importance of on-site response to complex disasters has also been emphasized [8, 9]. Internationally, most Americans are concerned that climate change will harm their health [83], and experts have warned that climate change is intensifying extreme weather events ahead of COP31 [90]. Specifically, climate change is reshaping relationships within the U.S. orange juice industry and aiding the spread of tropical diseases [91, 92, 96], and heatwave laborers in Indian salt pans are suffering from extreme heat [95], demonstrating its wide-ranging impacts.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Accelerating Carbon Neutrality Transition and Current Challenges
sections.content: As a forum is set to be held to diagnose ecosystem changes on the Korean Peninsula amidst the climate crisis [2], various policy discussions and challenges are emerging in the domestic transition to carbon neutrality. Representative Park Sang-woong has introduced an amendment to the 'Framework Act on Carbon Neutrality,' continuing efforts to strengthen the institutional foundation [14]. A significant change occurred in the AIDC Special Act, where LNG was excluded and renewable energy was included [62]. However, local political parties in Jeju, while agreeing on carbon neutrality goals, showed differing stances on the issue of gas power generation, indicating internal conflicts [13, 21]. Furthermore, concerns were raised regarding conflicts among local residents over the location of solar and wind power facilities [35], and potential environmental side effects from Energy Storage Systems (ESS), a core technology for carbon neutrality [36]. The fact that strengthened clean energy certification standards were 'rolled back,' leading to controversy over carbon accounting bubbles among big tech companies, once again highlights the importance of ensuring transparency [72].

sections.title: 🌱 Domestic Carbon Neutrality Practices and Technological Innovation
sections.content: Domestically, various technological innovations and community efforts are actively underway to achieve carbon neutrality. Investments in technology development continue, with Gongju National University selected for a carbon neutrality core technology development project [15, 26, 33, 48, 51], and KEPCO Research Institute shifting its focus to demonstration and commercialization-oriented R&D for carbon neutrality and digital transformation [18]. Gwangmyeong City's citizen-participatory carbon neutrality policy received an encouragement award, gaining recognition as an excellent case [12, 19, 24, 30], and Nonsan City is operating a carbon neutrality points system year-round to encourage citizen participation [20, 28, 31]. At the corporate level, Hyundai Motor is leading carbon neutrality efforts by utilizing V2G, a two-way electric vehicle charging technology, with EVs evolving into 'mobile ESS' [47, 75, 81]. Doosan Enerbility is accelerating shared growth through artificial intelligence and carbon neutrality [42]. Efforts to spread a culture of carbon neutrality practices are also steadily continuing in local communities and educational settings, including Seocho-gu's expansion of environmental education for all generations [10, 23, 49, 50], KEPCO MCS's plogging campaign [22], and Tangjeong Elementary School's milk carton collection activities [46].

sections.title: 🌱 Renewable Energy Expansion and Policy Trends
sections.content: Active movements by domestic local governments and public institutions to expand renewable energy supply are noticeably increasing. Gyeonggi Province has launched the 'Gyeonggi Energy Transition Working Group' with the goal of securing 10GW of renewable energy by 2030 [60, 69, 71, 77, 79]. Donghae City is pursuing a 2 billion KRW renewable energy convergence support project call for proposals and conducting demand surveys [56, 64, 68, 74, 78], establishing concrete regional-level plans. The Planning Office evaluated the Yeoju Haetbitdure Village solar power site as a significant success story for energy transition, raising expectations for renewable energy dissemination [57, 73]. Furthermore, efforts to introduce and expand various forms of renewable energy continue, including the signing of an MOU to promote public-led industrial complex solar power projects [76, 80], and the launch of a public-private development council to expand the supply of hydrothermal energy, primarily used in large and medium-sized commercial buildings, to residential complexes [82]. However, there are also calls from solar power operators for compensation for losses due to output restrictions [65], suggesting the need for policy enhancements to ensure stable renewable energy expansion.

sections.title: 🌍 International Climate Policy and Awareness
sections.content: The international community continues cooperation and discussions to address climate change. South Korea and Singapore have agreed to expand green transition and carbon market cooperation, strengthening their joint response to the climate crisis [3], and a vote on climate accountability is scheduled at the UN General Assembly [6]. An analysis suggesting the emergence of 'polarized climate multilateralism' as a legacy of COP30 illustrates the complexity of international climate cooperation [93]. Meanwhile, a request for proposals was issued in Jamaica for disaster mitigation in water facilities and strengthening climate change resilience [94], and local adaptation efforts continue, such as potato guardians in Peru fighting against climate change [97]. A new model for predicting plant resistance has been developed, expected to aid in climate change preparedness [86], and an environmental writer emphasized the importance of raising public awareness, arguing that media should make climate change more visible [84].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing has clearly illustrated the severity of the climate crisis, the multifaceted efforts being made domestically and internationally to overcome it, and the challenges that still persist. Sustained attention and action on climate change are more crucial now than ever. This concludes the climate briefing for May 15, 2026.
